page: Microsoft Azure DS14-8_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DSv2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz, featuring 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 112.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core. The processor is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. Storage is provided via a 229 GB local storage drive, and virtualization is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities and weaker single-core execution. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng (1245.69) and Geekbench (948.0) falls into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression register in the weak and poor categories respectively. However, multi-core benchmarks show moderate capacity, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5174.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 11028.38, both placing in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth is stable, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 591.72 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 74.36 GB/sec.

Given the high memory allocation of 112.0 GB relative to the 8 vCPUs, this server is positioned for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute performance. Database operations perform in the average tier, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 6439.18 and Redis SET operations reaching 10.21 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also moderate at 9.08 GB/sec. The instance is suitable for memory-optimized tasks, caching layers, and database hosting. However, it exhibits limitations in compute-heavy tasks; for instance, LLM inference with a medium 7B model drops to a weak 7.94 tokens/sec for text generation. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high memory density per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound applications, though users should note the tradeoff of lower single-core processing speeds.
description: The Microsoft Azure DS14-8_v2 is an x86_64 server instance within the DSv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. It features a high memory capacity of 112.0 GB (14.0 GB per core) and 229 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data reveals a tradeoff between weak single-core performance and average multi-core and memory capabilities. The instance achieves average scores in multi-core Geekbench (5174.0) and Passmark CPU (11028.38) tests, alongside stable memory bandwidth. With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and static web serving, though it is less suited for compute-heavy tasks or single-threaded processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS14-8_v2 is an x86_64 server featuring an Intel Xeon 8171M processor, 8 vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and 229 GB local storage, offering average multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 server featuring high memory density and dedicated cores, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
